text:
A motor vehicle may be equipped with:

(1) not more than two side cowl or fender lamps that emit an amber or white light without glare;

(2) not more than two running board courtesy lamps, one on each side of the vehicle, that emit an amber or white light without glare; and

(3) one or more backup lamps that:

(A) emit an amber or white light only when the vehicle is not moving forward; and

(B) may be displayed separately or in combination with another lamp.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 547. Vehicle Equip.
##### § 547.332 — Other Lamps Permitted

A motor vehicle may be equipped with:

    (1) not more than two side cowl or fender lamps that emit an amber or white light without glare;

    (2) not more than two running board courtesy lamps, one on each side of the vehicle, that emit an amber or white light without glare; and

    (3) one or more backup lamps that:

        (A) emit an amber or white light only when the vehicle is not moving forward; and

        (B) may be displayed separately or in combination with another lamp.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

not more than two running board courtesy lamps, one on each side of the vehicle, that emit an amber or white light without glare; and
one or more backup lamps that:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: emit an amber or white light only when the vehicle is not moving forward; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: may be displayed separately or in combination with another lamp.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
